steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Recognize complementary angles
steps.content: In a right triangle, the two acute angles add to $90$ degrees. Since angle $M$ and angle $N$ are complementary, $$\sin(M)=\cos(N).$$

steps.title: Transfer the given value
steps.content: The problem gives $$\sin(M)=\dfrac{\sqrt{35}}{6}.$$ Because $\cos(N)$ equals the sine of its complement, $$\cos(N)=\dfrac{\sqrt{35}}{6}.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: B is correct. The other choices use related but different trig ratios, such as a reciprocal or tangent-style expression, instead of the cofunction relationship.
